channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: NGINX Abuse Guard Module: Auto-Ban Scanners and Bots
item.description: Open your NGINX error log on any public server and you will see the same thing: a steady drip of 404s probing for /wp-login.php, /.env, /phpmyadmin, and a thousand other paths that do not exist on...

item.title: Build a Forum Site with GitHub Discussions (No Backend or Database)
item.description: Community forums are still one of the best ways to collect feedback, answer questions, and build discussions around a project. The problem is that most forum software comes with a lot of overhead....

item.title: Chat with Your Documents: Building a RAG Pipeline with AWS Blocks
item.description: One of the first features users expect from an AI application is deceptively simple: Upload a document. Ask questions. Get accurate answers. Whether you're building an internal knowledge portal,...

item.title: Laravel Request Lifecycle
item.description: 1. Request Entry Point The request begins at public/index.php. This file is the entry point for all incoming requests by your web server (Apache / Nginx) configuration. It loads the Composer...

item.title: Niti Sheet: The Spreadsheet Where Cells Become Full Nested Sheets – My New Open Alternative to Excel
item.description: I've built Niti Sheet (https://nitisheet.nitiware.com) — a powerful spreadsheet tool where any cell can turn into its own nested sheet. Perfect for complex data modeling, AI workflows, forms,...

item.title: Hookbox: a self-hosted webhook inbox with HMAC verification and replay
item.description: Hookbox: a self-hosted webhook inbox with HMAC verification and replay Debugging webhooks without a proper inbox is painful: ngrok tabs everywhere, lost payloads, no way to prove a signature was...

item.title: I built a self-hosted one-time secrets service in Node — here's the threat model
item.description: I built a self-hosted one-time secrets service in Node — here's the threat model Sharing an API key in Slack feels wrong. Password managers work great inside a team — less great when you need a...

item.title: The FBI reportedly won’t investigate ICE anymore
item.description: According to the New York Times, federal agents have been told that the FBI will no longer be investigating confrontations involving ICE agents. The DHS and DOJ denied the change in policy to The...

item.title: Why Your Git History Isn't Enough as Proof
item.description: Every developer has said it at least once: "check the git log — it's all there." Commit date, author, diff — it looks like an irrefutable timeline of who wrote what and when. It isn't. And if you...

item.title: How to Build Consistent UTM Links for Marketing Campaigns
item.description: UTM parameters are one of the simplest ways to understand where website traffic comes from. They help marketers identify which campaign, platform, post, advertisement, newsletter, or partner...

item.title: 🚑 Creating a Self-Healing Automation Framework with Playwright
item.description: 🧠 What is a Self-Healing Automation Framework? A self-healing test framework can automatically detect and recover from broken locators or test failures — reducing test flakiness and manual...

item.title: Orchid is a delightfully retro and approachable hipster synth
item.description: In 2017, I bought an old Magnus chord organ off Craigslist for $10. It's one of my favorite music gear purchases. Electric chord organs let you play full chords with just a press of a button,...

item.title: Birdfy’s solar-powered smart feeder is down to one of its best prices
item.description: Birdfy has kicked off a midyear sale, taking up to 40 percent off a range of its smart bird feeders. One of the best deals is on Netvue’s Birdfy Feeder AI-Powered Smart Bird Feeder with Camera,...

item.title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
item.description: "Everybody knows the Greeks are inside."

item.title: US Marshals arrest the Tate brothers in Miami
item.description: The manosphere influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate were arrested Saturday in Miami by US Marshals in relation to new rape and sex trafficking charges in England. According to the Associated Press,...

item.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
item.description: Current AI, a non-profit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat and more.

item.title: UAC-0145 Uses ClickFix CAPTCHAs to Infect Ukrainian Devices wih Malware
item.description: Russian state-sponsored threat actors have been observed leveraging the infamous ClickFix strategy to trick Ukrainian targets into infecting their own machines with data-stealing malware....

item.title: The Clapper was a bad smart home gadget — and a viral sensation
item.description: Clap on. Clap off. Well, more like, Clap, pause for half a beat but no longer because otherwise it'll stop hearing you, clap again because you waited too long, clap louder and faster, that didn't...

item.title: SonicWall SMA Zero-Days Exploited Before Disclosure to Gain Root Access
item.description: A previously undocumented threat actor has been attributed to the exploitation of recently disclosed SonicWall Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series VPN appliances as zero-days prior their public...

item.title: The future of physical games is not looking great
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on video games and physical media, follow Jay Peters. The Stepback arrives in our...

item.title: The grueling, 630-mile road race where the only fuel is sunlight
item.description: On July 19th, dozens of teams of high school students will begin a five-day, 630-mile road race from Fort Worth to Fort Stockton in Texas. But this is not your typical contest. The students design...

item.title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
item.description: Last year, Sam Altman invited author Dave Eggers to give a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. The man has written countless novels, screenplays, pieces of journalism, started McSweeney's, and...

item.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
item.description: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

item.title: Google might not kneecap the Pixel 11a with an old processor
item.description: Mystic Leaks suggests that the Pixel 11a will return to featuring a flagship-grade processor with the Tensor G6. Rather than the Tensor G5 found in the Pixel 10 and 10 Pro, the Pixel 10a shipped...

item.title: Google is open-sourcing its 3D emoji
item.description: Now, if you want to, you can use Google's 3D emoji in your own creations. The company shared some details about how it went about designing the little pictograms and why, as part of World Emoji...

item.title: Your Period Tracker Is (Probably) Spying on You
item.description: Plus: Russian cyberspies turn to infrastructure hacking, DHS repeatedly fails to realize it’d been hacked, a breach exposes an AI music generator’s scraping ways, and more.

item.title: How Google’s New Gemini Rates Work and How to Track Your Usage
item.description: Now that Google has changed how its usage quotas are tallied, you might not get as many AI responses as you did before.

item.title: Prompt Injection Attacks Are Thwarting AI Hacking Agents
item.description: “Context bombing” tricks malicious AI agents into shutting down before they can do harm.

item.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
item.description: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

item.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
item.description: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.
